title: C++ Preprocessor
content:
### Preprocessor

The preprocessor is a text-processing tool that runs before the compiler. It processes directives that start with `#`.

---

### Common Preprocessor Directives

| Directive | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `#include` | Insert file contents |
| `#define` | Define a macro |
| `#undef` | Undefine a macro |
| `#ifdef` / `#ifndef` | Check if macro defined |
| `#if` / `#elif` / `#else` / `#endif` | Conditional compilation |
| `#pragma` | Compiler-specific instructions |
| `#error` | Generate compile error |
| `#line` | Set line number |

---

### Include Guards

Prevent multiple inclusions of the same header:

```cpp
#ifndef MYHEADER_H
#define MYHEADER_H

// Header content

#endif
```

Or using `#pragma once` (supported by most compilers):

```cpp
#pragma once
```

---

### Conditional Compilation

```cpp
#ifdef DEBUG
  cout << "Debug: x = " << x << endl;
#endif

#if __cplusplus >= 202002L
  cout << "C++20 or later";
#endif
```

---

### Predefined Macros

| Macro | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `__cplusplus` | C++ standard version |
| `__LINE__` | Current line number |
| `__FILE__` | Current file name |
| `__DATE__` | Compilation date |
| `__TIME__` | Compilation time |
| `__FUNCTION__` | Current function name |

---

> **Key Point:** The preprocessor runs before compilation. It performs text substitution, file inclusion, and conditional compilation. Use `#pragma once` or include guards to prevent double inclusion.
